This protocol defines the internal bench test and inspection verification procedures, including the test methods, test configurations, DUTs, sample selection, sample-size rationale, acceptance criteria, and test records required to verify the applicable cleanability requirements identified in the Traceability Matrix [Ref. 1], in accordance with the approved IDS Design Verification Plan [Ref. 3].

This protocol defines the internal bench test and inspection verification procedures, including the test methods, test configurations, DUTs, sample selection, sample-size rationale, acceptance criteria, and test records required to verify the applicable cleanability requirements identified in the Traceability Matrix [Ref. 1], in accordance with the approved IDS Design Verification Plan [Ref. 3].

cells.value: When subjected 55.5% isopropyl alcohol (IPA), throughout the expected service life, the IDS materials and display glass shall maintain structural integrity without visible cracking or material degradation
604 - The system shall be cleanable using standard hospital cleaning and disinfectant agents without compromising its functional safety or integrity.
When subjected 27.3% ethanol, throughout the expected service life, the IDS materials and display glass shall maintain structural integrity without visible cracking or material degradation
When subjected 3% hydrogen peroxide, throughout the expected service life, the IDS materials and display glass shall maintain structural integrity without visible cracking or material degradation

When subjected to 1.1% sodium hypochlorite, throughout the expected service life, the IDS materials and display glass shall maintain structural integrity without visible cracking or material degradation

content.text: For each verification activity performed, the tester shall record execution-specific evidence sufficient to identify what was assessed, how it was assessed, and whether the acceptance criteria were met within a Test Record with a valid DRN as per SOP-001 Document & Data Control.

For each verification activity performed, the tester shall record execution-specific evidence sufficient to identify what was assessed, how it was assessed, and whether the acceptance criteria were met within a Test Record with a valid DRN as per SOP-001 Document & Data Control.

items.text: Test section and design requirement reference;
Date of assessment and tester name;
DUT identifiers, revision, serial number or unique sample ID, build state, and submitted-build configuration actually used;
Equipment identification and calibration or verification status where equipment status affects acceptance;
Exposure agent, exposure location, cycle timing, lighting condition, observations, photographs, and reviewed evidence;
Deviations, limitations, incomplete assessments, invalid test runs, and repeat-test rationale, where applicable; and
Pass/fail outcome against the applicable acceptance criteria.

content.text: Since all chemicals to be tested will be tested with the exact method a common test method has been developed in Section 3.3. Due to the nature of handling chemicals the following guidelines must be adhered to:

Since all chemicals to be tested will be tested with the exact method a common test method has been developed in Section 3.3. Due to the nature of handling chemicals the following guidelines must be adhered to:

items.text: When handling chemicals, it is mandatory to follow SOG-015 Chemicals document, wear required PPE (gloves, safety glasses, coat) and ensure the area of testing is within a well-ventilated area.
Each chemical exposure test will follow this test procedure exactly.
Different chemicals shall not be handled or used simultaneously.
Only one chemical type shall be present in the test area at a single time to avoid mixing or hazardous situations.
Chemicals and areas of the IDS to be tested must be labelled explicitly and immediately visible
No chemical shall be tested on an area of the IDS which has had a different chemical exposure to avoid mixing, and invalid results
In the event of accidental exposure or mixing of chemicals, the tester must immediately vacate the area and alert the WHS and Chemicals representative.

content.text: Mandatory Initial steps to follow before proceeding with any of the chemical solution mixing:
Mandatory Initial steps to follow before proceeding with any of the chemical solution mixing:

items.text: Confirm that chemical training has been completed
Confirm that SOG-015 has been read and understood
Read and understand the Safety Data Sheet for each chemical which can be found on the IDE server [Ref. 5] - [Ref. 8].
Storage of each chemical can be found within their risk assessment documents [Ref. 9]-[Ref. 12].
Ensure that the required PPE is being worn [Ref. 13].
Clean the working area, ensuring no other chemicals and obstructions are within the designated test area
Gather the chemical to be mixed
If the chemical concentration is not exactly the required testing concentration then gather the deionised water for mixing
Ensure measuring beaker and funnel are clean and dry with no residual solution or chemicals within.

items.text: Confirm the DUT is clean, dry, undamaged, and representative of the submitted build.
Define the mandatory exposure location at the corner of the IDS display so that the exposed area includes both enclosure material and display glass.

content.text: Figure 2: Intended Orientation & Setup of the IDS
Figure 2: Intended Orientation & Setup of the IDS

The exposure location shall be one corner of the IDS display such that the exposed area includes both enclosure material and display glass (Figure 2). Each chemical verification test shall be performed on a separate corner, or a different IDS sample, to avoid accidental mixture of chemicals. Different chemicals shall not be tested at the same time, on the same exposure area, or in any manner that could allow accidental mixing of chemicals.

The exposure location shall be one corner of the IDS display such that the exposed area includes both enclosure material and display glass (Figure 2). Each chemical verification test shall be performed on a separate corner, or a different IDS sample, to avoid accidental mixture of chemicals. Different chemicals shall not be tested at the same time, on the same exposure area, or in any manner that could allow accidental mixing of chemicals.

items.text: Use tape to border the exposed area to prevent external areas being affected by the chemical solution
Identify the adjacent unexposed control area for visual comparison.

Prepare the 55.5% IPA solution, lint-free cloths, plastic bag or polyethylene film, tape, timer, camera, and inspection lighting.If required, the chemical concentration can be achieved by mixing a pure higher concentration solution with distilled or deionised water.
Record DUT identification, build state, exposure location, control area, ambient conditions, and pre-test condition.

DR-4008 - IPA Chemical Compatibility: When subjected to 55.5% isopropyl alcohol (IPA), after two cycles of 10 minute wiping and a > 12 hour exposure cycle, the IDS materials and display glass shall maintain structural integrity without visible cracking or material degradation. [Ref. 1]

content.text: PASS: No visible cracking or signs of material degradation that compromises the intended function or usability of the device.
PASS: No visible cracking or signs of material degradation that compromises the intended function or usability of the device.

SAFETY WARNING: Do not mix cleaning or disinfecting agents. Mixing chemicals such as IPA, ethanol, hydrogen peroxide, and sodium hypochlorite can generate hazardous or toxic vapours. Always follow SOG-015 Chemicals.

SAFETY WARNING: Do not mix cleaning or disinfecting agents. Mixing chemicals such as IPA, ethanol, hydrogen peroxide, and sodium hypochlorite can generate hazardous or toxic vapours. Always follow SOG-015 Chemicals.
Take all precautions necessary to prevent exposure.
Take all precautions necessary to prevent exposure.

items.text: Perform only one chemical exposure test at a time unless physically separated and controlled to prevent mixing of chemicals.
Wear the required PPE (gloves, safety glasses, coat)
Perform test in ventilated environment

content.text: For the following method, ensure to record:
For the following method, ensure to record:

items.text: Chemical identity
Active ingredient percentage within the test solution
Exposure start and finish times for each wiping and soaked exposure
Photographs before and after test
Observations
Deviations throughout the test
Inspect and photograph the defined exposure area and the adjacent unexposed control area before exposure.

Cycle 1 wiping: Saturate a clean lint-free cloth with the specified chemical to be tested. Continuously wipe the defined exposure area for 10 minutes and ensure that both the display glass and enclosure material maintain a visibly wet surface, within the demarcated area, throughout the wiping period.

Cycle 1 soaked exposure: Saturate multiple paper cloths with the specified chemical. Place the cloths directly over the defined exposure area ensuring contact over the entire demarcated area. Ensure that the paper cloths are visibly saturated and wet with the solution.

Cover the cloths with a plastic bag or polyethylene film and secure the covering to the DUT using tape. Ensure that the tape is sealed around the edges of the plastic covering to minimise evaporation while maintaining continuous contact between the cloth and DUT.
Leave undisturbed for > 12 hours.

Remove the cloth and plastic covering after the Cycle 1 soaked exposure. Examine the exposed area and adjacent unexposed control area for cracking, crazing, whitening, colour change, gloss change, clouding, swelling, softening, tackiness, deformation, delamination, and any other visible material degradation. Take photographs and notes on any observations.
Repeat steps 2 to 6 for cycle 2.
Compare the exposed area against the area before exposure, after Cycle 1, and after Cycle 2 to assess effect of chemicals on device.

cells.value: Conditions to be met for the verification activity to pass.
Physical IDS assembly or defined configuration assessed during verification.
IDS assembly that is production-equivalent and representative of the submitted build state.
Risk-based requirement classification recorded in the Traceability Matrix.
The specific hardware, assembly state, labels, materials, surface finish, accessories, and settings relevant to the requirement being verified.
Defined procedure used to perform the verification activity.
Completed record of setup, reviewed files, observations, photographs, deviations, results, and pass/fail outcome.
